Discussing gender identity disclosure boundaries at work and safety planning

schedule.daily_routine: Commutes by Chuo Line, starts mornings with stretching/rehab exercises, tries to eat a quick breakfast to avoid blood-sugar swings, works mostly at the office for meetings but requests occasional remote days, and ends nights journaling incidents and preparing for the next day’s standup.

professional.company: Mid-sized Tokyo-based fintech startup building payment and risk tools for merchants (hybrid work, English/Japanese environment).
projects: Implementing fraud-detection feature flags and telemetry in a Go microservice
Maintaining a React dashboard for internal risk analysts
On-call improvements: alert tuning and runbook updates for production incidents
Data privacy compliance tasks (logging minimization and access audits)
professional.performance_issues: Focus and productivity have dipped due to stress and sleep disruption; a few missed minor deadlines and increased sick days. Sage is worried the situation will be used to undermine credibility if they report harassment.

Supportive teammate: Yuki Nakamura (helps shield Sage in meetings and offers to be a witness)

HR contact: Sayaka Mori (initially responsive, but Sage fears information leakage)

habits: Uses a private note app to log harassment incidents with timestamps and screenshots (password-protected)

lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a small studio apartment in Suginami; considering moving or staying temporarily with a cousin due to feeling unsafe and wanting distance from coworkers who know where they live.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to keep meals balanced and lower-glycemic to help with PCOS (more protein/vegetables, fewer sugary drinks), but stress sometimes leads to skipped meals and convenience-store dinners.
